name: Build Pipeline with tsup for TypeScript Libraries
description: Set up a fast build pipeline using tsup to produce ESM, CJS, and declaration files for publishable TypeScript packages.

steps.title: Install tsup
steps.description: Add tsup as a dev dependency for fast, zero-config TypeScript bundling.

steps.title: Create tsup configuration
steps.description: Define the build config for dual ESM and CJS output with declarations.
steps.tip: Set minify: true for production libraries distributed via CDN.

steps.title: Configure package.json for dual output
steps.description: Set exports, main, module, and types fields for both ESM and CJS consumers.
steps.command: npm pkg set main='./dist/index.cjs' module='./dist/index.js' types='./dist/index.d.ts' && npm pkg set exports['.'].import='./dist/index.js' exports['.'].require='./dist/index.cjs' exports['.'].types='./dist/index.d.ts'

steps.title: Add build scripts
steps.description: Add build and dev scripts to package.json.

steps.title: Run the build
steps.description: Execute the build and verify the output files.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# TypeScript Build Pipeline with tsup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Installing tsup ==="
pnpm add -D tsup

echo "=== Creating tsup.config.ts ==="
cat > tsup.config.ts << 'EOF'
import { defineConfig } from 'tsup';

export default defineConfig({
  entry: ['src/index.ts'],
  format: ['esm', 'cjs'],
  dts: true,
  splitting: true,
  sourcemap: true,
  clean: true,
  minify: false,
  treeshake: true,
  outDir: 'dist',
});
EOF

echo "=== Adding scripts ==="
npm pkg set scripts.build="tsup"
npm pkg set scripts.dev="tsup --watch"

echo "=== Building ==="
pnpm build

echo ""
echo "=== Output ==="
ls -la dist/

faq.question: Why tsup over tsc for building?
faq.answer: tsup uses esbuild under the hood, which is 10-100x faster than tsc. It handles ESM/CJS dual output, tree-shaking, and bundling in a single tool. tsc is still used for type checking (tsc --noEmit) but not for building.

faq.question: What is the difference between ESM and CJS output?
faq.answer: ESM (ES Modules) uses import/export syntax and is the modern standard. CJS (CommonJS) uses require/module.exports and is needed for older Node.js code. Publishing both ensures your library works everywhere.

faq.question: How do I handle CSS or assets in tsup?
faq.answer: tsup does not process CSS by default. For libraries with CSS, use the css: true option to extract CSS files, or use a separate tool like postcss for CSS processing.
